digest.top_line: ENZARAD: enza added to definitive RT+ADT missed 1° MFS (8yr 74 vs 72%, HR 0.88, p=0.34); benefit isolated to cN1/pelvic-RT subgroup (MFS HR 0.47).
digest.tldr: Prostate carried the day. Adding enzalutamide to a definitive RT+ADT backbone was overall null (MFS HR 0.88), but the cN1/pelvic-RT subgroup saw MFS HR 0.47 (0.29-0.76). Lower baseline risk vs STAMPEDE (11% vs 39% cN1) explains the muted overall effect; hypothesis-generating for node-positive pelvic-RT candidates.

studies.name: ENZARAD (ANZUP 1303)
studies.tldr: MFS 8yr 74% vs 72%, HR 0.88 (0.67-1.15) p=0.34; enza on an RT+ADT backbone missed 1° endpoint, benefit isolated to cN1/pelvic-RT.

details.text: 📊 Efficacy (8-yr rates, enza vs conventional NSAA)
📊 Enzalutamide benefit by prespecified subgroup (MFS / OS HRs) — the RT-actionable read

details: 🔍 Phase III RCT, N=802, 8 countries (2014-2018), median f/u 8yr; enza 160mg×24mo vs active NSAA×6mo on identical 2yr-LHRH + EBRT backbone

subdetails: Prostate 78Gy, or 46Gy + brachytherapy boost (8% got brachy)
Pelvic nodes 46Gy elective + boost to gross nodes
Pelvic RT required for cN1; optional cN0, declared pre-randomization (40% planned)
QA: credentialing, real-time review first 5 plans/site, 20% sampled after

details: 🔍 Baseline: 90% Gleason 8-10, 36% PSA >20, 12% cN1 by conventional imaging

details.text: 🔗 vs STAMPEDE (abiraterone): lower baseline risk explains ENZARAD's weaker overall effect

details: ⚠️ Control arm was active NSAA (6mo), not placebo; enza's edge measured vs a real antiandrogen
⚠️ Subgroup benefit prespecified but hypothesis-generating; pelvic-RT arm enriched for N1 (28% vs 0%), Gleason 9-10 (62% vs 49%)
⚠️ OS was original 1° endpoint, switched to MFS mid-trial for fewer-than-expected deaths

verdict.rationale: Phase 3, prespecified MFS primary, 8yr f/u, active-NSAA control; negative overall reinforces restrained intensification, cN1/pelvic-RT benefit mirrors STAMPEDE. Subgroups prespecified, not practice-defining alone.

studies.significance: The intensification benefit tracks the pelvic-RT-planned subgroup (MFS HR 0.47, OS HR 0.53), but that arm was enriched for cN1 (28% vs 0%), so nodal burden, not the RT field itself, likely drives it. Planning whole-pelvis RT for cN1 disease is where adding enzalutamide over 6mo NSAA earns its place; cN0 pts gained nothing.
studies.monday_clinic: In high-risk localized prostate with cN1 disease or planned pelvic-nodal RT, the data support enzalutamide intensification over 6mo NSAA; it does not extend to cN0 pts, who showed no MFS benefit.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 3 open-label RCT, N=802 across 8 countries (2014-2018), median follow-up 8 years. **Primary: metastasis-free survival** (switched from OS mid-trial for fewer-than-expected deaths). Enzalutamide 160mg × 24mo vs conventional NSAA × 6mo, both on 24mo LHRH agonist + EBRT.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: High-risk clinically-localized or locally-advanced prostate, EBRT-suitable. **90% Gleason 8-10, 36% PSA >20, 12% cN1** by conventional imaging. 40% planned pelvic RT, 8% brachytherapy boost.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Prostate to **78Gy, or 46Gy + brachy boost**. Pelvic nodal RT 46Gy elective + boost to gross nodes, **required for cN1**, optional (pre-declared) for cN0. Intensive QA: credentialing, real-time review of first 5 plans per site, 20% sampling thereafter.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Primary MFS **not met**; PFS positive, OS neutral; benefit isolated to the cN1 and pelvic-RT-planned subgroups (see tables).

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: The cN1 MFS benefit mirrors STAMPEDE abiraterone; the **weaker overall effect** reflects ENZARAD's more favorable baseline risk (lower cN1 fraction, lower PSA, less cT3-4).

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents high-risk localized or locally-advanced prostate treated with definitive EBRT plus 2yr ADT, node-positive pts benefiting most. Does not represent cN0 low-nodal-burden or very-high-risk disease, where enzalutamide added no MFS benefit.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Active NSAA control (not placebo) narrows the measured enzalutamide effect. Subgroup benefits are prespecified but hypothesis-generating, and the pelvic-RT arm is confounded by nodal enrichment. Primary endpoint switched from OS to MFS mid-trial.

open_questions: Which pelvic-RT subgroups drive the enzalutamide benefit
Biomarkers to identify who needs treatment intensification
Does nodal RT sterilization enable enzalutamide's distant-metastasis benefit

papers.title: ESMO 2025: Randomized Phase III Trial of Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) with Radiation Therapy with or without Enzalutamide for High Risk, Clinically Localized Prostate Cancer: ENZARAD (ANZUP 1303)
papers.abstract: ESMO 2025 ENZARAD (ANZUP 1303), randomized phase II trial of ADT + radiation therapy +/- enzalutamide, localized prostate cancer.
